risk_title: Political Instability Preventing Effective Project Implementation
risk_summary: The extraordinary political situation in Afghanistan has made effective project implementation impossible, leading to cancellation of trust fund financing and suspension of IDA grants.
evidence_quote: The Afghanistan Reconstruction Trust Fund (ARTF) and Energy Sector Management Program (ESMP) trust fund were canceled because the project could not be effectively implemented under the current extraordinary situation in Afghanistan. Undisbursed IDA grants under this project will remain suspended until the World Bank decides.

risk_summary: Political and governance risks remain rated as High across all assessment periods, indicating persistent fragility in the government's ability to implement the project effectively.
evidence_quote: Risk Category: Political and Governance - Rating at Approval: High, Previous Rating: High, Current Rating: High

risk_title: Institutional Capacity Collapse Under Conflict Conditions
risk_summary: Institutional capacity for implementation and sustainability remains rated High risk, reflecting the breakdown of government systems under the current extraordinary situation.
evidence_quote: Risk Category: Institutional Capacity for Implementation and Sustainability - Rating at Approval: Substantial, Previous Rating: High, Current Rating: High

risk_title: Significant Reduction in Project Commitment Due to Political Crisis
risk_summary: The Afghanistan operation commitment was substantially reduced from $113 million to $60 million, reflecting the impact of the political crisis on project viability.
evidence_quote: Also, the Afghanistan operation commitment of $113 million was reduced to $60 million.
